essence: Builds and assembles mechanical components and machinery using precision tools, technical drawings, and quality‑controlled processes.

identity.title: Mechanical Assembler
identity.summary: Assembles mechanical parts, sub‑assemblies, and machinery using technical drawings, tools, and manufacturing standards across engineering and industrial environments.

salary.notes: Assemblers specialising in precision engineering, aerospace, or high‑tolerance manufacturing can exceed £45k-£55k+.

qualifications: Training in mechanical assembly, engineering, or manufacturing
Ability to read technical drawings and follow assembly instructions
Knowledge of mechanical components, fasteners, and tools
Understanding of quality control, tolerances, and safety procedures

lifestyle.description: Mechanical assemblers work in manufacturing plants, engineering workshops, and industrial facilities. They build mechanical systems, follow precise assembly procedures, and ensure components meet quality standards. The role suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on work, precision, and building physical systems.

habits: Uses hand tools, power tools, and measurement equipment
Performs quality checks and ensures correct tolerances
Documents assembly steps and reports issues
